Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules]\n- Backend: [e.g., Node.js, PostgreSQL, Prisma]\n- Node version: [e.g., 20.x]\n- Package manager: [npm / pnpm / yarn]\n\n---\n\n## Key Commands\n\n```\nnpm run dev          — start dev server\nnpm run build        — production build\nnpm test             — run test suite\nnpm run lint         — lint\nnpm run typecheck    — TypeScript check (run after every change)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nsrc/\n  components/    — reusable UI components\n  pages/         — route-level pages\n  lib/           — utilities and shared logic\n  styles/        — global styles and design tokens\npublic/          — static assets\n```\n\n---\n\n## Core Files\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| `src/lib/api.ts` | Central API client |\n| `src/components/Layout.tsx` | Root layout and nav |\n| `.env.example` | All required env vars (copy to `.env.local`) |\n\n---\n\n## Non-Negotiables\n\n- Never commit secrets, API keys, or `.env` files.\n- Never skip typechecking — run `npm run typecheck` after every non-trivial change.\n- Never add a dependency without checking bundle impact first.\n- No `console.log` in committed code.\n- `main` is protected — work on feature branches, open a PR for review.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Notes\n\n- [Describe high-level data flow]\n- [Note hard constraints]\n- [Call out seams that are easy הדבקה על זכוכיות גוניור\n\n[One-line description of the project goal and target audience]\n\n---\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- Framework: [e.g., Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript]\n- Styling: [e.g., Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules]\n- Backend: [e.g., Node.js, PostgreSQL, Prisma]\n- Node version: [e.g., 20.x]\n- Package manager: [npm / pnpm / yarn]\n\n---\n\n## Key Commands\n\n```\nnpm run dev          — start dev server\nnpm run build        — production build\nnpm test             — run test suite\nnpm run lint         — lint\nnpm run typecheck    — TypeScript check (run after every change)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\nsrc/\n  components/    — reusable UI components\n  pages/         — route-level pages\n  lib/           — utilities and shared logic\n  styles/        — global styles and design tokens\npublic/          — static assets\n```\n\n---\n\n## Core Files\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| `src/lib/api.ts` | Central API client |\n| `src/components/Layout.tsx` | Root layout and nav |\n| `.env.example` | All required env vars (copy to `.env.local`) |\n\n---\n\n## Non-Negotiables\n\n- Never commit secrets, API keys, or `.env` files.\n- Never skip typechecking — run `npm run typecheck` after every non-trivial change.\n- Never add a dependency without checking bundle impact first.\n- No `console.log` in committed code.\n- `main` is protected — work on feature branches, open a PR for review.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Notes\n\n- [Describe high-level data flow]\n- [Note hard constraints]\n- [Call out seams that are easy to break]\n\n---\n\n## Known Quirks\n\n- [Surprising behaviors — high-value institutional {"parentUuid":"de6faf4c-4dc1-4d13-881e-b7d65ca96c09","isSidechain":false,"message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01SRUyUVzFawcNT8AHCSof5D","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"The user is asking two questions:\n1. What's the perfect way to set up security, rules, agents, memory to get maximum benefit but work efficiently?\n2. If they have a new task, should they continue in the same chat or open a new folder, and if a new folder, where?\n\nLet me answer both questions clearly and concisely.\n\n**Question 1: Perfect architecture for security/rules/agents/memory**\n\nThis is about Claude Code's architecture. The key components are:\n- **Security**: hooks that block destructive commands, install-review before installing skills\n- **Rules**: CLAUDE.md + rules/ files (lean, always-loaded vs on-demand)\n- **Agents**: Stop hooks for code review and session recording, scheduled agents like Saturday research runs\n- **Memory**: MEMORY.md as an index with individual memory files loaded on demand\n\nThe real pattern here is organizing by scope and load strategy. Global CLAUDE.md holds identity and non-negotiables, project-level CLAUDE.md keeps things lean and specific, memory gets indexed but files are read when needed, skills only activate when called, and hooks respond to events. This layered approach lets you have comprehensive rules without bloating every interaction.\n\nFor security, I'm using a PreToolUse hook to block destructive bash commands, an install-review rule in the global CLAUDE.md, and SessionStart reminders about critical rules. On the practical side, staying in the same chat works for the same project, but switching to a different folder and Claude Code session makes sense when moving to a completely different project.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"cache_creation_input_tokens":146,"cache_read_input_tokens":87825,"output_tokens":1355,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":146,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"not_available","iterations":[{"input_tokens":3,"output_tokens":1355,"cache_read_input_tokens":87825,"cache_creation_input_tokens":146,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":146},"type":"message"}],"speed":"standard"},"diagnostics":null},"requestId":"req_011Cbq9A1hXVB9EpKk9pxUt6","type":"assistant","uuid":"8f92a986-3628-4c73-949e-828707562b12","timestamp":"2026-06-08T04:13:16.597Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"claude-vscode","cwd":"c:\\Users\\yaniv\\ליו","sessionId":"3040aa33-1674-4681-95a2-62b20384ba8e","version":"2.1.168","gitBranch":"master"}

שאלות נפרדות — אענה על כל אחת:\n\n---\n\n## 1. ארכיטקטורה מושלמת\n\nהמודל הנכון הוא **שכבות לפי תדירות שימוש:**\n\n```\nתמיד נטען          → Global CLAUDE.md (זהות + non-negotiables)\n                    → Project CLAUDE.md (lean — רק מה שספציפי לפרויקט)\n                    → MEMORY.md (אינדקס בלבד)\n\nבעת אירוע          → Hooks (Stop/PreToolUse/SessionStart)\n\nלפי דרישה          → Rules files (reference material)\n                    → Memory files (נקראים כשרלוונטיים)\n                    → Skills (נטענים רק כשמופעלים)\n```\n\n**אבטחה:** שלוש שכבות:\n- PreToolUse חוסם פקודות הרסניות\n- install-review לפני כל התקנה\n- SessionStart מזכיר את הכללים הקריטיים\n\n**מה שיש לך עכשיו** — כבר קרוב מאוד לזה. הדבר היחיד שחסר הוא למלא את CLAUDE.md עם ה-tech stack האמיתי ברגע שיוחלט.\n\n---\n\n## 2. אותו צ'אט או תיקיה חדשה?\n\n| מצב | מה עושים |\n|---|---|\n| משימה חדשה **באותו פרויקט** | שיחה חדשה באותה תיקיה (`ליו`) |\n| פרויקט אחר לגמרי | תיקיה חדשה תחת `C:\\Users\\yaniv\\` |\n\n**שיחה חדשה** = context נקי, אבל אותו settings.json + CLAUDE.md + memory. זה מספיק לרוב המשימות.\n\n**תיקיה חדשה** = רק אם זה קודבייס אחר, מטרה אחרת, rules אחרים.\n\n---\n\nאז לשאלה שלך — אם המשימה החדשה היא עדיין על פרויקט גוניור: שיחה חדשה באותה תיקיה. מה complex\n\n**Beep, PreToolUse, and SessionStart reminder:**\n- Beep and the destructive command blocker are universal and should be global\n- The SessionStart reminder is currently project-specific with Hebrew rules, but could move to global if the content applies universally\n\n**For the session recorder specifically:**\n- The memory path depends on the project's directory hash, so it needs to would let the hook work across projects without hardcoding paths\n\n**The cleaner split is:**\n- Global hooks handle what applies everywhere: the beep on stop, destructive command blocking, code review, and universal session reminders\n- Project-specific hooks handle what varies: the session recorder with its dynamic memory path, typecheck commands that depend on the tech stack, and any project-specific session setup\n- Need to avoid duplication if the code review agent ends up in both global and local settings\n\n**The real question is:** what should consistently happen in every coding session regardless of which project I'm in? I could also have it compute the memory directory path dynamically using the project root, but that's getting complicated. Let me step back and give a straightforward recommendation instead: move beep, the destructive command blocker, and code review to global settings since they're universal, while keeping the TypeScript typecheck project-specific since it depends on the project's setup.\n\nFor new projects, running `/init` would copy the session recorder and typecheck hooks from existing settings, and since the path is relative to each project's unique hash, it would automatically write to the right location. But I'm realizing the hardcoded path in my session recorder is specific to this project's hash—a different project would need a different path entirely. So the session recorder either stays project-specific with each project getting its own hook, or I need to make it smart enough to figure out the right path dynamically.\n\nI think the cleaner approach is a three-tier architecture: global hooks that work everywhere (beep, the PreToolUse blocker, and the Hebrew reminders), a project template tier with the session recorder and typecheck hooks that gets copied to each new project, and then project-specific content like CLAUDE.md and memory files.
